title: farfugium japonicum var. formosanum
intro: Farfugium japonicum var. formosanum, also known as the Formosa Farfugium or Golden Raydor, is a perennial plant native to Taiwan and parts of East Asia, characterized by its large, golden-yellow leaves and tall stalks. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The Formosa Farfugium is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues, as well as supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
